response:
Sec. 1  
peanuts are a type of food that contains proteins which can trigger allergic reactions in some people  
the immune system of individuals with peanut allergies reacts to the proteins in peanuts and can cause severe reactions  
allergies are caused by the body's immune system overreacting to substances that are normally harmless  
this overreaction can lead to symptoms such as hives, swelling, difficulty breathing and even anaphylaxis  
the body cannot process or tolerate the proteins in peanuts in the same way it processes other foods  
this is why people with peanut allergies cannot eat peanuts or any food that contains peanuts  

Sec. 2  
underwater environments do not contain the proteins found in peanuts and are not a source of peanut allergens  
the human body is not adapted to survive in water and the physical conditions of underwater environments are different from land  
the allergic reaction to peanuts is not related to the environment or the presence of water  
the body's immune system does not recognize water as a source of allergens  
the physical properties of water do not affect the allergic reaction to peanuts
